pages.raw_text:
The official committee of IL
drafting a new city charter made
Roadway last night on the provis.
ions relating to franchises. While
the work was not finished, tenta
tive agreement was reached on
many of the most difficult prob.
lens, and there is prospect Of an
eventual agreement by the whole OF
the commission on the entire prop.
osition. The question of the com.
pensation for franchises Is still left
open and recommitted to the com.
mittee.


Universal transfers were killed
by vote of eight to four, the four
votes for the transfers being cast
by the labor representatives. AS
agreed upon for the time being, the
provisions as to franchises are:


Publication of ordinances for four
weeks at expense of applicant.


No monopolies.


Franchises for power lines ex.
ceeding 10000 volts shall specify
streets to which they apply, any
other franchises to state definite
bounds.


No capital stock to be issued on
account of franchise values.


Franchises shall not be renewed
sooner than three years prior to ex.
piration.


Franchises limited to 25 years
except that steam railroads and in
terurban lines may be given sucn
franchises as four-fifths vote may
designate.


Provision for sworn annual re-
ports of franchise holders, with
privilege of examination of books
under penalty of 500 a day.


Street railroads shall pay not less
than five per cent per annum on
one-third estimated cost of uncon.
structed bridges, or cost of bridges
already in operation.


Bonds for grantee or lessees OF
